description: Papers on wearable sensor data processing methods including imputation, signal quality assessment, missing data handling, sensor fusion for activity recognition, preprocessing pipelines, non-wear detection, foundation models (NormWear, LSM), device calibration, and demographic biases. Covers 2020-2026.

papers.label: Toward Foundation Model for Multivariate Wearable Sensing of Physiological Signals (NormWear)
papers.authors: Yunfei Luo, Yuliang Chen, Asif Salekin, Tauhidur Rahman
papers.headline: First multimodal wearable foundation model (NormWear) using channel-aware attention with CLS token, pretrained on PPG/ECG/EEG/GSR/IMU, evaluated across 11 datasets and 18 applications.
papers.relevance: Core reference for NormWear 768-dim embeddings from PPG/accelerometer. Directly relevant to the next-step Sensor Foundation Model goal.

papers.label: Toward World Modeling of Physiological Signals with Chaos-Theoretic Balancing and Latent Dynamics (NormWear-2)
papers.authors: Yunfei Luo, Xi Chen, Yuliang Chen, Lanshuang Zhang, Md Mofijul Islam, Siwei Zhao, Peter Kotanko, Subhasis Dasgupta, Andrew Campbell, Rakesh Malhotra, Tauhidur Rahman
papers.headline: NormWear-2 extends the foundation model approach to world modeling; forecasts physiological signals across multiple time scales using chaos-theoretic balancing and latent state transition adaptation.
papers.relevance: Direct successor to NormWear; demonstrates forecasting and intervention-conditioned modeling for physiological time series.

papers.label: Scaling Wearable Foundation Models (LSM)
papers.authors: Girish Narayanswamy, Xin Liu, Kumar Ayush, Yuzhe Yang, Xuhai Xu, Shun Liao, Jake Garrison, Shyam Tailor, Jake Sunshine, Yun Liu, Tim Althoff, Shrikanth Narayanan, Pushmeet Kohli, Jiening Zhan, Mark Malhotra, Shwetak Patel, Samy Abdel-Ghaffar, Daniel McDuff
papers.headline: LSM: a multimodal foundation model trained on 40M hours of wearable sensor data from 165K people. Establishes scaling laws for imputation, interpolation, and extrapolation.
papers.relevance: Key reference for wearable foundation model scaling; includes imputation as a core task; largest wearable-signals dataset to date.

papers.label: Physical activities enable scalable foundation modelling for broad-spectrum health prediction (StepFM)
papers.authors: Zhenghuang Wu, Yuyao Zhu, Songlin Xu
papers.headline: StepFM: a foundation model built solely on step counter data. Privacy-preserving, computation-efficient. Transferred to >20 health risk prediction tasks.
papers.relevance: Demonstrates low-dimensional step data as viable foundation model input with privacy benefits.

papers.label: Inertia-1: An Open Exploration of Wearable Motion Foundation Models
papers.authors: Zongzhe Xu, Aakarsh Anand, Sarah Jiang, Chuntung Zhuang, Zitao Shuai, Sriram Sankararaman, Yuzhe Yang
papers.headline: Comprehensive exploration using >18.2M hours of accelerometer data. Evaluates data choices, model choices, and training choices across 15 datasets.
papers.relevance: Provides design recipes for motion foundation models; key resource for building accelerometer-based foundation models.

papers.label: SiamQuality: A ConvNet-Based Foundation Model for Imperfect Physiological Signals
papers.authors: Cheng Ding, Zhicheng Guo, Zhaoliang Chen, Randall J Lee, Cynthia Rudin, Xiao Hu
papers.headline: SIAMQUALITY uses Siamese self-supervised learning on 36M PPG pairs for robust representations. CNN backbone outperforms transformers on imperfect physiological data.
papers.relevance: Directly addresses signal quality in foundation model training; robust to noisy and incomplete physiological data.

papers.label: A robust PPG foundation model using multimodal physiological supervision
papers.authors: Eloy Geenjaar, Vince Calhoun, Scott Daly, Gouthaman KV, Lie Lu, Trisha Mittal, Daniel P. Darcy
papers.headline: PPG foundation model leveraging ECG/respiratory signals for contrastive pretraining. Handles noisy PPG without requiring high-quality pretraining data.
papers.relevance: Multimodal supervision for robust PPG representation; improved 14/15 downstream tasks with 3x fewer subjects.

papers.label: Multiple Imputation Approaches for Epoch-level Accelerometer data in Trials
papers.authors: Mia S. Tackney, Elizabeth Williamson, Derek G. Cook, Elizabeth Limb, Tess Harris, James Carpenter
papers.headline: Epoch-level (5-second) missing data framework for accelerometer trials. Parametric and non-parametric MI approaches. Validated on PACE-UP Trial.
papers.relevance: Directly addresses missing accelerometer data in clinical trials with epoch-level granularity.

papers.label: A framework for handling missing accelerometer outcome data in trials
papers.authors: Mia S. Tackney, Derek G. Cook, Daniel Stahl, Khalida Ismail, Elizabeth Williamson, James Carpenter
papers.headline: Analysis framework using wear time to define missingness on the epoch level; wear-time-informed MI for accelerometer outcomes.
papers.relevance: Practical framework for non-wear and missing data handling in accelerometer-based trials.

papers.label: DynImp: Dynamic Imputation for Wearable Sensing Data Through Sensory and Temporal Relatedness
papers.authors: Zepeng Huo, Taowei Ji, Yifei Liang, Shuai Huang, Zhangyang Wang, Xiaoning Qian, Bobak Mortazavi
papers.headline: DynImp uses nearest-neighbor feature-axis imputation plus LSTM-based denoising autoencoder. Tested at >50% missing rates.
papers.relevance: Handles extreme missingness by leveraging cross-sensor correlations and temporal dynamics.

papers.label: VCR: Learning Valid Contextual Representation for Incomplete Wearable Signals
papers.authors: Yuxuan Weng, Wenhan Luo, Qijia Shao
papers.headline: Self-supervised framework using orthogonal tokenizer and missing-aware Mixture-of-Experts. Reconstructs only shared components to avoid hallucination.
papers.relevance: Novel missing modality handling for wearable data; avoids hallucinating non-inferable modality details.

papers.label: Self-Supervised and Topological Signal-Quality Assessment for Any PPG Device
papers.authors: Wei Shao, Ruoyu Zhang, Zequan Liang, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
papers.headline: First fully unsupervised PPG SQA combining contrastive 1D-ResNet with topological data analysis. Cross-device without re-tuning.
papers.relevance: Unsupervised quality assessment working across heterogeneous PPG devices; no labeled data required.

papers.label: Lightweight ResNet-Based Deep Learning for Photoplethysmography Signal Quality Assessment
papers.authors: Yangyang Zhao, Matti Kaisti, Olli Lahdenoja, Jonas Sandelin, Arman Anzanpour, Joonas Lehto, Joel Nuotio, Jussi Jaakkola, Arto Relander, Tuija Vasankari, Juhani Airaksinen, Tuomas Kiviniemi, Tero Koivisto
papers.headline: Lightweight ResNet with Squeeze-and-Excitation for PPG SQA. >99% parameter reduction; 96.52% AUC on M4M dataset.
papers.relevance: Efficient PPG quality assessment suitable for on-device wearable deployment.

papers.label: Reduction of motion artifacts from photoplethysmography signals using learned convolutional sparse coding
papers.authors: Giulio Basso, Xi Long, Reinder Haakma, Rik Vullings
papers.headline: Algorithm unfolding combines signal decomposition with deep learning for PPG motion artifact removal. SNR improved from -7.07 to 11.23 dB.
papers.relevance: Interpretable motion artifact removal using learned dictionaries; validated on PPG-DaLiA.

papers.label: SpO2 Predictor-Guided Stage-Wise Time-Frequency Reconstruction of Low-Quality Dual-Wavelength PPG
papers.authors: Zequan Liang, Elahe Hosseini, Ning Miao, Mahdi Pirayesh Shirazi Nejad, Wei Shao, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
papers.headline: Stage-wise time-frequency reconstruction for low-quality dual-wavelength PPG, guided by pretrained SpO2 predictor.
papers.relevance: Novel PPG reconstruction preserving physiologically relevant SpO2 information beyond waveform fidelity.

papers.label: Versatile Cardiovascular Signal Generation with a Unified Diffusion Transformer (UniCardio)
papers.authors: Zehua Chen, Yuyang Miao, Liyuan Wang, Luyun Fan, Danilo P. Mandic, Jun Zhu
papers.headline: UniCardio: multi-modal diffusion transformer for PPG/ECG/BP signal denoising, imputation, and translation in a unified generative framework.
papers.relevance: Unified approach to cardiovascular signal quality improvement; handles denoising and imputation jointly.

papers.label: DEM: A Distilled Explanation Model for Interpretable Anomaly Detection in Physiological Sensor Networks
papers.authors: Jyotirmoy Singh, Anushka Roy, Shreea Bose, Chittaranjan Hota
papers.headline: Glass-box anomaly detection distilling XGBoost into interpretable decision trees. Handles false alarms from missing data and sensor faults.
papers.relevance: Handles missing data and sensor faults with interpretable outputs for WBANs.

papers.label: MuteBench: Modality Unavailability Tolerance Evaluation for Incomplete Multimodal Fusion
papers.authors: Wugeng Zheng, Ziwen Kan, Tianlong Chen, Chen Chen, Song Wang
papers.headline: Benchmark evaluating 6 fusion architectures under modality missing and within-modality missing across 9 clinical datasets (125K samples).
papers.relevance: Comprehensive benchmark for missing modalities in clinical AI including wearable contexts.

papers.label: Leveraging Imperfect Medical Data: A Manifold-Consistent Spatio-Temporal Network for Sensor-based HAR (MCSTN)
papers.authors: Jiangtao Fan, Anish Jindal, Amir Atapour-Abarghouei
papers.headline: MCSTN handles missing measurements, sensor failures, and noise via dual-level corruption modeling and manifold consistency. PAMAP2, Opportunity, WISDM.
papers.relevance: Robust HAR under imperfect sensing addressing real-world wearable sensor failures.

papers.label: Decomposing and Fusing Intra- and Inter-Sensor Spatio-Temporal Signal for Multi-Sensor WHAR (DecomposeWHAR)
papers.authors: Haoyu Xie, Haoxuan Li, Chunyuan Zheng, Haonan Yuan, Guorui Liao, Jun Liao, Li Liu
papers.headline: DecomposeWHAR: decomposes multi-sensor signals into intra- and inter-sensor components using depthwise separable conv, SSM, and self-attention.
papers.relevance: Novel decomposition-fusion paradigm modeling intra- and inter-sensor relationships separately.

papers.label: A Comparison of Fusion Techniques for Multi-Modal HAR on the HARMES Dataset
papers.authors: Ahmed Mohamady, Robin Burchard, Kristof Van Laerhoven
papers.headline: Systematic comparison of 7 fusion techniques (IMU + audio + humidity). Gated multimodal fusion achieves macro F1=0.82.
papers.relevance: Head-to-head comparison; practical guidance for sensor fusion in multi-modal wearables.

papers.label: FairTune: A Bias-Aware Fine-Tuning Framework Towards Fair Heart Rate Prediction from PPG
papers.authors: Lovely Yeswanth Panchumarthi, Saurabh Kataria, Yi Wu, Xiao Hu, Alex Fedorov, Hyunjung Gloria Kwak
papers.headline: Fine-tuning PPG foundation models can widen gender fairness gaps despite reducing MAE up to 80%. GroupDRO and inverse frequency weighting mitigate this.
papers.relevance: Critical finding about demographic bias amplification during wearable sensor FM fine-tuning.

papers.label: Evidence for Phenotype-Driven Disparities in Freezing of Gait Detection and Approaches to Bias Mitigation
papers.authors: Timothy Odonga, Christine D. Esper, Stewart A. Factor, J. Lucas McKay, Hyeokhyen Kwon
papers.headline: HAR models for FOG detection exhibit bias across age, sex, disease duration, and FOG phenotype. Transfer learning improves fairness.
papers.relevance: Shows demographic and clinical biases in wearable sensor models; transfer learning as mitigation.

papers.label: A Bayesian Functional Accelerated Failure-Time Model with Varying Effects Correcting for Measurement Error
papers.authors: Joseph Yang, Roger Zoh, Carmen Tekwe, Lan Xue
papers.headline: Bayesian functional AFT model addressing measurement error in wearable data. Models subgroup effects across race/region for stroke risk.
papers.relevance: Statistical framework for measurement error correction in wearable accelerometer data with demographic heterogeneity.

papers.label: Lightweight Cross-Device Sleep Tracking on the WeBe Wearable Platform
papers.authors: Wei Shao, Ehsan Kourkchi, Krishi Prashant Shah, Zequan Liang, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun
papers.headline: Lightweight sleep pipeline from raw accelerometer using globally calibrated threshold. Cross-device validation (WeBe vs ActiGraph). TST error 27.4 min.
papers.relevance: Cross-device calibration for accelerometer sleep tracking; simple, reproducible pipeline.

papers.label: BCoughBench: Benchmarking Respiratory Acoustic Foundation Models Under Body-Coupled Wearable Sensor Conditions
papers.authors: Mayur Sanap, Prasanna Desikan, Edgar Lobaton
papers.headline: Benchmarks 5 respiratory FMs under body-coupled wearable conditions. Mean AUROC declines from 0.785 to 0.689-0.723. Sex classification collapses.
papers.relevance: Negative result: FM performance degrades substantially under realistic wearable sensor conditions.

papers.label: Collaborative HAR with Passive Inter-Body Electrostatic Field
papers.authors: Sizhen Bian, Vitor Fortes Rey, Siyu Yuan, Paul Lukowicz
papers.headline: Evaluates novel electrostatic field sensing for HAR. Negative result: less competitive than accelerometer alone.
papers.relevance: Negative result demonstrating novel sensing modality underperforms vs standard accelerometer for HAR.
